content_blocks.raw_text: YEAR 6
1. Dimension 1: Self-awareness

- A student can draw three pictures of themselves in different roles and can talk about the different ways they behave in each of these roles. This is me at home. This is me at school. This is me with my friends. Here I am...
- Can identify three things they are good at, three qualities they have and three strengths they think they might need in the future.
- Can say three things that are important to them and why they are important.
2. Dimension 2: Emotional knowledge
- Knows about the continuum of emotions. Can take part in a discussion about when you might be in control of an emotion and when it controls you and say what makes a difference.
- Can take part in a discussion about how the arts (music/dance and drama) can impact on how someone feels.
3. Dimension 3: Emotional skills — regulation, expression and resilience
heading_path: 3. Dimension 3: Emotional skills — regulation, expression and resilience

content_blocks.raw_text: - Can identify four different ways to deal with anger and decide which is the preferred option and why.
heading_path: 3. Dimension 3: Emotional skills — regulation, expression and resilience

content_blocks.raw_text: - Can identify at least five words showing different aspects of the following emotions: happiness; sadness; interest; anger.
heading_path: 3. Dimension 3: Emotional skills — regulation, expression and resilience

content_blocks.raw_text: - Knows how thinking can affect how you feel and says what thinking strategies are useful.
heading_path: 3. Dimension 3: Emotional skills — regulation, expression and resilience

content_blocks.raw_text: 4. Dimension 4: Shared humanity
- Can write about ways in which everyone is unique and the reasons it is valuable.
- Can say what 'The Golden Rule' is, how it appears in all major religions and what it means in practice.
5. Dimension 5: Interpersonal skills
- Demonstrates the 'rules' of good conversation — a balance between talking and listening.
- Demonstrates regular small acts of kindness — goes out of their way to help others.
- Works well within a group to achieve a given target.
6. Dimension 6: Situational skills — empathy and awareness of others
heading_path: 6. Dimension 6: Situational skills — empathy and awareness of others

content_blocks.raw_text: - Shows an understanding of what someone might feel if they: were bullied; lost something or someone; broke a leg; failed a test; were hungry or tired.
heading_path: 6. Dimension 6: Situational skills — empathy and awareness of others

content_blocks.raw_text: - Can view a film and identify the feelings of characters within a situation by their voice tone, body posture and facial expression.
heading_path: 6. Dimension 6: Situational skills — empathy and awareness of others

content_blocks.raw_text: 7. Dimension 7: Leadership — goal setting and personal confidence
heading_path: 7. Dimension 7: Leadership — goal setting and personal confidence

content_blocks.raw_text: - Demonstrates an interest in getting better at something. Understands the concept of personal bests.
heading_path: 7. Dimension 7: Leadership — goal setting and personal confidence

content_blocks.raw_text: - Is aware of those who have been left out and includes them.
heading_path: 7. Dimension 7: Leadership — goal setting and personal confidence

content_blocks.raw_text: - Accepts mistakes as part of learning.
heading_path: 7. Dimension 7: Leadership — goal setting and personal confidence

content_blocks.raw_text: 8. Dimension 8: Promoting the positive — solution focus and thankfulness
heading_path: 8. Dimension 8: Promoting the positive — solution focus and thankfulness

content_blocks.raw_text: - Identifies three things to be grateful for and three things to look forward to.
heading_path: 8. Dimension 8: Promoting the positive — solution focus and thankfulness

content_blocks.raw_text: - Can identify one situation in which positive thinking helped and can talk/write about the difference it made.
heading_path: 8. Dimension 8: Promoting the positive — solution focus and thankfulness

content_blocks.raw_text: 9. Dimension 9: Dealing with conflict — assertiveness and negotiation
heading_path: 9. Dimension 9: Dealing with conflict — assertiveness and negotiation

content_blocks.raw_text: - Stands up for others who are less able to stand up for themselves.
heading_path: 9. Dimension 9: Dealing with conflict — assertiveness and negotiation

content_blocks.raw_text: - Knows when a situation is not safe and leaves.
heading_path: 9. Dimension 9: Dealing with conflict — assertiveness and negotiation

content_blocks.raw_text: 10. Dimension 10: Repair and restoration
- Knows about responsibility pie-charts: how much was down to me, how much to someone else, how much to chance.
- Offers to make up for harm caused.
11. Dimension 11: Ethics, integrity and human rights
heading_path: 11. Dimension 11: Ethics, integrity and human rights

content_blocks.raw_text: - In a small group, talks about why people don't always get what they deserve, and what this means for how we see the world and others in it.
heading_path: 11. Dimension 11: Ethics, integrity and human rights

content_blocks.raw_text: - In small groups, using the Strengths in Circles cards on inclusion and equity, chooses one from each set and decides how this will be put into practice in the classroom.
heading_path: 11. Dimension 11: Ethics, integrity and human rights

content_blocks.raw_text: 12. Dimension 12: Meaning and spirituality
- Chooses a symbol or picture that illustrates what really matters in life and talks about the choice.
- Identifies someone who is a role model for how to live life well and researches what that person did.
